libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For version 2.0.0 use jakarta.activation.* imports, not javax.activation.*. Tests should exercise public Jakarta Activation APIs with local test data such as temp files, in-memory DataSource/CommandObject implementations, and classpath resources; no Docker service, environment variable, or system property is required.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The API jar POM states it is intended only for compile-time use and omits implementation-specific classes. jdeps shows jakarta.activation-api references missing com.sun.activation.registries classes, and instantiating MimetypesFileTypeMap fails with NoClassDefFoundError without this implementation j
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 1.2.1 tests and metadata mention javax.activation; blindly reusing them for 2.0.0 will keep javac failures or stale reachability entries.

The implementation jar contains overlapping jakarta.activation classes as well as the missing com.sun.activation registries; Gradle/native-image verification remains authoritative for any classpath ordering or duplicate-class issues.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0 is an API-only artifact; normal Activation use of MimetypesFileTypeMap/MailcapCommandMap/DataHandler reaches com.sun.activation implementation classes and default mailcap/mimetype resources that are not in the API jar.
